prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG wuxia world atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Hearts of Wulin / Jianghu inspired wuxia map, continent plus archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye Chinese ink-wash atlas view of one dominant jianghu continent with a large uneven offshore archipelago.
Subject: one main martial-world continent with complex coastlines, misty gulfs, bays, capes, narrow straits, broken rocky coasts, river deltas, inland lakes, and many irregular island chains offshore. Islands must be unevenly distributed with varied sizes and shapes: temple islands, tiny hermit islets, craggy islands, reef rings, lagoons, narrow channels, remote atolls, hidden sect islands, pirate coves, sacred waterfall islands, and forbidden manual sanctuaries. Include mist-covered mountain peaks, bamboo forest valleys, ancient temple complexes, river market towns, hidden sect strongholds, imperial roads, terraced farmland, desert borderlands, wetlands, tundra highlands, forests, and coastal regions.
Style/medium: Chinese ink wash continent map, jianghu locations in classical painting style, elegant hand-painted atlas, wuxia drama sourcebook cartography, subtle flowing chi energy trails as non-text decorative lines.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, dominant continent offset to one side and archipelago sweeping diagonally around it, no simple grid spacing, full map visible.
Lighting/mood: melodramatic wuxia epic, honor and betrayal, forbidden love, wandering heroes, drifting mist, secret sea routes, classical Chinese landscape beauty.
Color palette: ink black, soft mineral greens, mist grey, pale parchment, muted river blue, cinnabar red accents, light gold temple roofs.
Materials/textures: rice paper, brush ink coastlines, watercolor washes, calligraphic terrain strokes without readable characters, delicate seal-like non-text symbols.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable Chinese characters, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Islands should feel natural and irregular, not evenly spaced.
